task_summary: SP21b second reviewer follow-up: --format human --form cat is revised from a stylized cyber/block icon into a literal coarse-grid conversion of repo-root cat.png: square white face with thick stepped black outline, blocky pointed ears, vertical rectangular eyes, tiny centered square nose, slim standing body, two narrow legs/feet, and a large stepped curled right tail. State variation is limited to final-row markers outside the common silhouette.

verification_evidence.summary: 34 passed after applying the literal coarse-grid cat.png sprite, focused tests, and README wording.

implemented_contract.reference_image_inspected: cat.png was freshly re-inspected with the work-session read_image tool #1037 before this reviewer-follow-up edit. The observed reference traits were monochrome coarse pixel art, a mostly square white head/face with thick black stepped outline, pointed ears at the top corners, two vertical rectangular eyes, a tiny centered square nose, a slim standing body with narrow legs/feet, and a large stepped curled tail on the right.
implemented_contract.cat_form_pixel_sprite: --format human --form cat now prepends a terminal-friendly block-cell sprite that approximates those traits with a common coarse-grid silhouette rather than slash-heavy line art or invented cyber shapes.
implemented_contract.cat_state_variation: Idle, attentive, coding, waiting, and blocked presence states keep the same head/body/tail silhouette. Only the final row status marker changes, so the state marker does not alter the reference-like shape.
implemented_contract.default_form_preserved: The implicit human renderer and --form default remain the existing terminal human surface without cat-only headings.
implemented_contract.state_json_preserved: The state JSON renderer remains selected only by --format state and is not rewritten for terminal form presentation.
implemented_contract.html_preserved: The HTML renderer and html output/watch behavior remain out of scope for sprite changes.
implemented_contract.watch_preserved: Bounded human watch with --form cat continues to print one cat terminal surface per foreground iteration without JSONL record markers.
implemented_contract.live_and_launcher_safety_preserved: The slice does not add mew connection work and does not change live-active-window, live-desk, hidden/background/network safety fields, or launcher execution gates.

covered_cases: implicit human output equals --form default output
cat output is distinct from default and includes terminal form: cat metadata
cat output includes stepped pointed ears, square face/head outline, vertical rectangular eyes, tiny nose, slim body, two narrow legs/feet, and a stepped curled right tail
cat output no longer includes the prior slash-heavy line-art fragments or embedded code label
idle, attentive, coding, waiting, and blocked presence states share one common silhouette with only final-row markers differing
human --watch-count with --form cat prints two cat terminal surfaces for two iterations
human cat watch stdout omits JSON record_type and schema_version markers
README describes the cat.png coarse pixel conversion and keeps the --format human --form cat --watch-count usage command

acceptance_notes: The implementation is scoped under experiments/mew-ghost only.
The cat terminal form remains a presentation layer for --format human; it does not alter the underlying state payload.
No mew connection work is included in this slice.
This report is local side-dogfood evidence for the SP21b practical slice.
remaining_risk: No broad mew connection, HTML, state JSON, live-read, or launcher behavior change was attempted; SP21b remains limited to the human cat terminal sprite polish slice.
